matches.triviaFact: Sehwag smashed a blistering century to set up India's series-levelling win
matches.playerHighlight: Virender Sehwag's explosive innings at the top set the tone

matches.triviaFact: Zaheer Khan's devastating spell with the new ball dismantled New Zealand's batting lineup
matches.playerHighlight: Zaheer Khan picked up crucial wickets to seal the series for India

matches.triviaFact: Brett Lee's fiery pace was too much for Bangladesh as Australia dominated at home
matches.playerHighlight: Brett Lee terrorized the Bangladesh batsmen with raw pace

matches.triviaFact: Flintoff showed his all-round prowess in testing conditions in Dhaka
matches.playerHighlight: Andrew Flintoff delivered with both bat and ball to earn Man of the Match

matches.triviaFact: Chris Gayle lit up Harare with a commanding all-round display
matches.playerHighlight: Chris Gayle dominated with bat and ball against Zimbabwe

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2003 Super Six
matches.triviaFact: Shane Bond took 6/23 — the best figures in a WC match against Australia — but it wasn't enough as Brett Lee's 5/42 helped Australia win
matches.playerHighlight: An extraordinary bowling duel between Bond and Lee at Port Elizabeth

matches.triviaFact: Andrew Symonds scored 91* and took a crucial run-out as Australia beat Sri Lanka to reach yet another World Cup Final
matches.playerHighlight: Symonds anchored Australia's chase with an aggressive unbeaten knock at Port Elizabeth

matches.triviaFact: Sourav Ganguly led from the front with 111* as India demolished Kenya by 91 runs to reach the World Cup Final for the first time since 1983
matches.playerHighlight: Ganguly's captain's knock set up a huge total as India booked their ticket to the Final at Durban

matches.triviaFact: Ricky Ponting hammered an unbeaten 140 off 121 balls as Australia demolished India by 125 runs at the Wanderers to win their third consecutive World Cup
matches.playerHighlight: Ponting's aggressive century was the cornerstone of Australia's dominant 359/2, the highest World Cup Final total at the time

matches.triviaFact: Ian Bradshaw's dramatic 34* off 36 balls from 147/8 guided West Indies to a 2-wicket win over England in one of the greatest CT Final finishes
matches.playerHighlight: Bradshaw and Browne's last-wicket stand of 71 pulled off the impossible at The Oval

matches.triviaFact: Shoaib Malik's all-round display powered Pakistan to a win in the Paktel Cup
matches.playerHighlight: Shoaib Malik contributed with both bat and ball in a polished all-round performance

matches.triviaFact: Sangakkara's brilliant 89* steered Sri Lanka to a comfortable win in the VB Series Final
matches.playerHighlight: KC Sangakkara anchored the chase with an unbeaten 89 off 93 balls

matches.matchContext: Sri Lanka in Bangladesh ODI Series 2006
matches.triviaFact: Aftab Ahmed's explosive batting powered Bangladesh to a memorable 4-wicket victory over Sri Lanka in Bogra
matches.playerHighlight: Aftab Ahmed's aggressive knock turned the chase in Bangladesh's favor

matches.matchContext: Australia in South Africa ODI Series 2006
matches.triviaFact: The greatest ODI match ever — South Africa chased down 434, the highest successful chase in ODI history. Gibbs hit 175 off 111 balls as SA scored 438/9
matches.playerHighlight: Herschelle Gibbs smashed 175 off 111 balls with 21 fours and 7 sixes, leading the most famous ODI chase in history at the Wanderers

matches.triviaFact: Mahela Jayawardene's brilliant 115* guided Sri Lanka past New Zealand and into the World Cup Final
matches.playerHighlight: Jayawardene's unbeaten century was a masterclass in ODI batting under pressure in Jamaica

matches.triviaFact: Glenn McGrath's 3/18 helped Australia restrict South Africa to 149 in a completely one-sided semi-final in St Lucia
matches.playerHighlight: McGrath produced a vintage performance in what would be one of his final ODI matches

matches.triviaFact: Adam Gilchrist smashed 149 off 104 balls in the World Cup Final — later revealing he used a squash ball inside his glove for grip
matches.playerHighlight: Gilchrist's blistering knock remains the highest individual score in a World Cup Final

matches.triviaFact: Shane Watson's 105* guided Australia to a comfortable 6-wicket win over New Zealand in the Champions Trophy Final at Centurion
matches.playerHighlight: Watson's unbeaten century in the final sealed Australia's second Champions Trophy title

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2011 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Virender Sehwag smashed 175 off just 140 balls — the highest WC score at the time — as India crushed Bangladesh in the tournament opener at Mirpur
matches.playerHighlight: Sehwag's explosive century set the tone for India's eventual World Cup triumph on home soil

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2011 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: One of the rarest results in WC history — a tie between India and England in Bangalore. Andrew Strauss scored 158 but India's batting made it a dead heat
matches.playerHighlight: Strauss hit 158 in a losing (well, tying) cause as both teams finished on identical scores in a thrilling group match

matches.triviaFact: Jacob Oram's 4/39 dismantled South Africa as New Zealand won a close quarter-final at Mirpur
matches.playerHighlight: Oram's bowling spell was the difference as New Zealand knocked out South Africa

matches.triviaFact: Tillakaratne Dilshan scored a century and took 2/38 as Sri Lanka thrashed England by 10 wickets in Colombo
matches.playerHighlight: Dilshan's all-round display led to a comprehensive Sri Lankan victory, one of the most dominant WC QF performances

matches.triviaFact: Kumar Sangakkara scored a masterful unbeaten 54 as Sri Lanka chased down New Zealand's target to reach the World Cup Final in Mumbai
matches.playerHighlight: Sangakkara's composed knock guided Sri Lanka through a tricky chase in Colombo

matches.triviaFact: The biggest cricket match on earth — India vs Pakistan in a World Cup semi-final at Mohali, with Sachin Tendulkar's 85 anchoring India's run chase
matches.playerHighlight: Tendulkar survived early dropped catches and played a crucial knock as India beat their arch-rivals to reach the World Cup Final at home

matches.matchContext: India vs South Africa ODI Series 2010
matches.triviaFact: Sachin Tendulkar became the first player in history to score a double century in ODIs — 200* off 147 balls at Gwalior
matches.playerHighlight: Tendulkar's historic 200* included 25 fours and 3 sixes, rewriting the record books

matches.triviaFact: Ravindra Jadeja's 2/24 and his crucial 33* with the bat helped India defend 129 to beat England by 5 runs in a low-scoring Champions Trophy Final
matches.playerHighlight: Jadeja's all-round performance in dire conditions at Edgbaston won India their second Champions Trophy

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2015 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Virat Kohli's 107 was the centerpiece of India's 76-run victory over Pakistan in the biggest group-stage rivalry match at Adelaide
matches.playerHighlight: Kohli's century continued his superb record against Pakistan in World Cups

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2015 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Chris Gayle's 215 off 147 balls — the first-ever double century in a World Cup match — destroyed Zimbabwe at Canberra
matches.playerHighlight: Gayle's record-breaking double century included 10 fours and 16 sixes in an utterly dominant display

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2015 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: AB de Villiers smashed 162 off 66 balls — reaching his century off just 31 balls, the fastest in WC history — as SA won by a record 257 runs
matches.playerHighlight: De Villiers' innings featured 17 fours and 8 sixes in the most destructive WC batting performance ever seen

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2015 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: An all-time classic — Trent Boult took 5/27 and Mitchell Starc 6/28, but NZ chased 151 with 1 wicket to spare. Kane Williamson and Boult saw them home
matches.playerHighlight: Boult's brilliant 5-wicket haul bowled Australia out cheaply before NZ scraped home in a thriller at Auckland

matches.triviaFact: Rohit Sharma's 137 and Umesh Yadav's 4/31 powered India past Bangladesh at the MCG
matches.playerHighlight: Rohit's assured century set up a comfortable Indian win in the quarter-final

matches.triviaFact: Martin Guptill smashed 237* — the highest World Cup score ever — as New Zealand obliterated West Indies by 143 runs in Wellington
matches.playerHighlight: Guptill's record-breaking double century featured 24 fours and 11 sixes in a stunning quarter-final display

matches.triviaFact: Grant Elliott hit a six off Dale Steyn's penultimate ball to seal New Zealand's first-ever World Cup Final berth, breaking South African hearts at Eden Park
matches.playerHighlight: Elliott's unbeaten 84 in a tense chase will forever be remembered as one of cricket's most dramatic semi-final knocks

matches.triviaFact: James Faulkner took 3/36 as Australia restricted New Zealand to 183, then cruised to victory at the MCG in front of 93,000 fans
matches.playerHighlight: Faulkner's death-overs bowling strangled New Zealand's innings in the biggest match of the tournament

matches.triviaFact: Fakhar Zaman's 114 powered Pakistan to a stunning 180-run victory over India in the Champions Trophy Final at The Oval — one of the biggest upsets in ICC tournament history
matches.playerHighlight: Fakhar's fearless century on the biggest stage humbled favorites India in a shocking result

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2019 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Aaron Finch hit 100 as Australia beat England by 64 runs at Lord's in a crucial group-stage encounter
matches.playerHighlight: Finch's composed century laid the foundation for Australia's statement win at the home of cricket

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2019 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Jonny Bairstow's 111 powered England to a must-win victory over India at Edgbaston, keeping their World Cup hopes alive
matches.playerHighlight: Bairstow's aggressive century set the tone for England's crucial chase

matches.triviaFact: Chris Woakes' 3/20 and Jason Roy's 85 helped England dismantle defending champions Australia at Edgbaston to reach the Final
matches.playerHighlight: Woakes' new-ball spell removed Australia's top order as England cruised to a comfortable semi-final victory

matches.triviaFact: Jos Buttler's 150 off 77 balls and Chris Gayle's 162 off 97 balls made this one of the most explosive bilateral ODIs ever played
matches.playerHighlight: Buttler's incredible 150 was matched by Gayle's blistering 162, producing 652 runs in a single match

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: David Warner blasted 163 off 124 balls — his highest ODI score — to set up Australia's win over Pakistan at Bengaluru
matches.playerHighlight: Warner's career-best 163 featured 17 fours and 5 sixes in a batting masterclass

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Mohammed Shami took 5/54 on a turning Dharamsala pitch as India beat New Zealand early in the tournament
matches.playerHighlight: Shami's 5-wicket haul continued India's dominance in the 2023 World Cup

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Glenn Maxwell smashed 106 off just 44 balls — one of the fastest WC centuries ever — as Australia obliterated Netherlands by 309 runs in Delhi
matches.playerHighlight: Maxwell's blistering 106 off 44 balls included 8 fours and 8 sixes in a devastating display of power hitting

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Rohit Sharma's blistering 87-ball century set the tone as India maintained their unbeaten run against England in Lucknow
matches.playerHighlight: Rohit's attacking century was the highlight of India's dominant group-stage campaign

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2023 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Virat Kohli scored his 49th ODI century — equaling Sachin Tendulkar's all-time record — in a dominant Indian win over South Africa at Kolkata
matches.playerHighlight: Kohli's historic century drew him level with Tendulkar's seemingly unbreakable record

matches.triviaFact: Mohammed Shami took 7/57 — the best-ever World Cup semi-final figures — as India crushed New Zealand by 70 runs at the Wankhede
matches.playerHighlight: Shami's devastating spell ripped through New Zealand's batting, with India's bowlers ensuring a comfortable semi-final victory

matches.triviaFact: Travis Head scored a match-winning 51-ball 62* as Australia chased down South Africa's total with 3 wickets and 6 balls to spare at Eden Gardens
matches.playerHighlight: Head's calm aggression in the chase secured Australia's place in the final despite a shaky start

matches.triviaFact: Travis Head's stunning 137 off 120 balls silenced 100,000 fans at Ahmedabad as Australia won their 6th World Cup title, denying unbeaten India
matches.playerHighlight: Travis Head counter-attacked from 47/3, putting on 192 with Labuschagne to chase down 241 with 7 overs to spare

matches.triviaFact: Virat Kohli delivered a vintage performance as India knocked out Australia in the Champions Trophy semi-final at Dubai
matches.playerHighlight: Kohli's match-winning display guided India past Australia and into the Champions Trophy Final

matches.triviaFact: Rachin Ravindra's all-round brilliance powered New Zealand past South Africa in the Champions Trophy semi-final at Lahore
matches.playerHighlight: Ravindra's match-winning performance earned New Zealand a spot in the Champions Trophy Final

matches.triviaFact: Rohit Sharma's unbeaten century sealed India's Champions Trophy triumph over New Zealand in Dubai
matches.playerHighlight: Rohit's match-winning knock in the final capped a dominant Indian campaign in the 2025 Champions Trophy

matches.triviaFact: The most dramatic ODI final ever — tied match, tied Super Over, decided on boundary countback. Stokes' unbeaten 84* kept England alive after being 86/4
matches.playerHighlight: Ben Stokes played one of cricket's greatest innings under pressure, surviving a deflection off his bat for 6 overthrows in the final over

matches.triviaFact: Dhoni promoted himself above Yuvraj Singh and sealed India's second World Cup with an iconic six over long-on at the Wankhede Stadium
matches.playerHighlight: MS Dhoni's unbeaten 91 off 79 balls, capped by the winning six, became one of cricket's most replayed moments

matches.matchContext: India vs Sri Lanka ODI Series 2014
matches.triviaFact: Rohit Sharma smashed 264 off 173 balls — the highest individual ODI score in history, surpassing his own record of 209
matches.playerHighlight: Rohit Sharma hit 33 fours and 9 sixes in a breathtaking display of batting at Eden Gardens, Kolkata

matches.triviaFact: New Zealand's Matt Henry dismantled India's top order, removing Rohit, Kohli, and Rahul to derail the tournament favorites
matches.playerHighlight: Matt Henry's devastating spell of 3/37 broke the back of India's chase in the semi-final at Old Trafford

matches.triviaFact: Steve Smith's brilliant 105 anchored Australia's chase as they knocked out India at the SCG to reach the final
matches.playerHighlight: Steve Smith's composed century guided Australia past India in a dominant semi-final performance

matches.matchContext: ICC Cricket World Cup 2007 Group Stage
matches.triviaFact: Matthew Hayden blasted a brutal century as Australia demolished New Zealand by 215 runs in a completely one-sided encounter
matches.playerHighlight: Matthew Hayden's explosive innings set up Australia's massive total

matches.triviaFact: Yuvraj Singh's all-round brilliance — 57* with the bat and 2/44 with the ball — powered India past Australia in the quarter-final at Ahmedabad
matches.playerHighlight: Yuvraj Singh delivered a match-winning all-round performance as India knocked out the defending champions
